summary: Partially decompiled the start of the fighter common `ft_0BEC` range by moving several routines and literals out of `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` into new `src/melee/ft/ft_0BEC.c` and adding `ft_0BEC.h` plus the new object to `obj_files.mk`. The PR report shows the new file at 98.76% overall, with `ftCo_800BECB0`, `ftCo_800BEF04`, and trivial stubs matching 100%, while `ftCo_800BED88` remained partial at 97.34%. Supporting changes exposed item factory prototypes, renamed Fox special attribute type usage to `ftFox_DatAttrs`, and flattened `Fighter` offset `0x2228` bitfields from `x2228_flag.bN` to direct `x2228_bN` names.
what_changed: Added `src/melee/ft/ft_0BEC.c` with C implementations or conditional C/ASM bodies for `ftCo_800BECB0`, `ftCo_800BED84`, `ftCo_800BED88`, `ftCo_800BEF00`, `ftCo_800BEF04`, and `ftCo_800BEFD0`.
Removed the corresponding assembly and sdata2 literals `ftCo_804D8C00`, `ftCo_804D8C04`, `ftCo_804D8C08`, and `ftCo_804D8C0C` from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`.
Added `src/melee/ft/ft_0BEC.h` with address-commented declarations for the moved functions and additional `ftCo_800BF...` / `ftCo_800C...` declarations still in the broader range.
Included `ft/ft_0BEC.h` from `ftCo_Init.c` and `ftdemo.c`, and removed old declarations from `ftdemo.h`.

Modeled the routines as common fighter motion/item setup: call `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, set `x2219_b2`, `x2219_b1`, and `x2228_b1`, clear `item_gobj` and `x1984_heldItemSpec`, then branch on fighter kind to spawn Fox blaster gun, Dr. Mario pills, or Ness bat using item constructors.
Updated item headers to expose needed constructors: `it_802AE994` for Fox blaster, `it_802C09C4` for Dr. Mario pills, and `it_802AD590` for Ness bat.
Renamed Fox special attribute type from `ftFoxAttributes` / `_ftFoxAttributes` to forward-declared `ftFox_DatAttrs`, and propagated that type through Fox init and special move files.

Changed `Fighter` offset `0x2228` from a nested `x2228_flag` struct to direct bitfields `x2228_b0` through `x2228_b7`, with `x2228_b3` kept as a 2-bit field; updated uses across common fighter, cliff, draw, lib, Fox, and Sandbag files.
smart_moves: Kept unmatched or fragile routines under `ASM` with `#if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP)` C bodies and embedded fallback assembly, allowing readable C progress without breaking strict matching builds.
Used explicit `LITERAL static ATTRIBUTE_USED` constants for small float literals moved from assembly, preserving sdata2/literal behavior needed by matching.
Separated the newly decompiled range into its own `ft_0BEC.c` object while leaving the rest of `ft_08A4.s` in assembly, which is a low-risk incremental migration pattern.
Introduced real prototypes for item creation functions rather than relying on implicit or placeholder signatures, making the fighter-item interaction in the C translation type-checkable.
Renamed `ftFoxAttributes` to `ftFox_DatAttrs`, matching an existing project naming style where fighter DAT attributes are named `*_DatAttrs`.
Flattened `x2228` bitfields to direct `fp->x2228_bN` access, which likely made bitfield codegen and callsite readability closer to neighboring fighter flags.
decomp_lessons: For partial file matches, it is acceptable to move a small contiguous function range to C and keep remaining functions in the old assembly file, as long as `obj_files.mk` preserves object order.

When decompiling fighter common routines that spawn held items, first identify the motion-state call, common flag writes, held-item pointer clears, and fighter-kind switch; the item constructor calls can then be typed from item headers.

C translation may require struct cleanup outside the target file: matching `ftCo_800BECB0`/`ftCo_800BED88`/`ftCo_800BEF04` depended on usable `Fighter` bitfields, Fox DAT attribute typing, and item factory prototypes.

Literal float precision matters: this PR used `0.71428`/`0.71428f` for the Dr. Mario pill scale multiplier and `0.8`/`0.8f` for the Ness bat scale multiplier, corresponding to the removed sdata2 values.
Use `FtPart_RThumbNb` instead of raw part index `0x31` when the assembly clearly passes the same fighter part to `ftParts_8007500C`; the symbolic enum documents the item attach point.
Trivial `blr` functions can become empty C functions, as shown by `ftCo_800BED84`, `ftCo_800BEF00`, and `ftCo_800BEFD0` matching 100%.
Do not overclaim full file completion from a high report score: the PR title and report explicitly call this a partial match because `ftCo_800BED88` still had a nonzero score.
naming_conventions: `ftCo_800BECB0` style names were retained for unknown common fighter functions, with address comments in the header.
New header guard for `ft_0BEC.h` used the address-style `GALE01_0BECB0` pattern.
Fox DAT attributes were normalized to `ftFox_DatAttrs`, with a forward declaration in `ftFox/forward.h`.
Fighter bitfields at offset `0x2228` were named directly as `x2228_b0`, `x2228_b1`, `x2228_b2`, `x2228_b3`, `x2228_b5`, `x2228_b6`, and `x2228_b7`, replacing `x2228_flag.bN`.
Item prototypes in headers were annotated with address comments such as `/* 2AE994 */`, `/* 2C09C4 */`, and `/* 2AD590 */`.
`LITERAL` was added in `placeholder.h` as `static ATTRIBUTE_USED` for file-local constants that must survive optimization/linking.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed 234 lines from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` but left later symbols such as `ftCo_800BEFD4` in assembly, showing a range-splitting tactic.
Used conditional embedded assembly with `nofralloc` and `#pragma peephole on` after fallback blocks for functions that were not safe to rely on as pure C under `MUST_MATCH`.
Added `#ifdef MUST_MATCH u8 _[8] = { 0 }; #endif` unused stack padding in translated functions where stack layout influenced matching.
Preserved motion-state float arguments using local literals `ftCo_804D8C00` and `ftCo_804D8C04` for `0` and `1`.
Matched item scale calculations as `fp->x34_scale.y * fp->co_attrs.model_scaling`, with additional multipliers for Dr. Mario pill (`0.71428f`) and Ness bat (`0.8f`).
The PR report recorded `ftCo_800BECB0` and `ftCo_800BEF04` at 100%, `ftCo_800BED88` at 97.34%, and the new file overall at 98.76%.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
Because there was no reviewer discussion, there is no direct evidence of reviewer intent beyond the merged state and the PR report.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match `ftCo_800BED88` or remove its embedded assembly fallback.
Search for additional migrations out of `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` after PR #989.
Audit all remaining `x2228` bitfield uses to ensure they use direct `x2228_bN` names rather than `x2228_flag.bN`.
Look for later naming refinements for `ftCo_800BECB0`, `ftCo_800BED88`, and `ftCo_800BEF04` once their gameplay purpose was identified.
Check whether `LITERAL` remained the preferred pattern for static used float constants in later matching work.
